title: Bobby Doesnt Live Here Anymore

jokes.text: Latka guessing exactly right about the beach ball being 'Red, green, yellow and blue'
jokes.setup: Tony asks Latka to guess what color the beach ball is
jokes.punchline: Latka immediately guesses correctly with specific colors
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of Latka being impossibly good at guessing, establishing the comedic pattern

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Latka, I want you to guess what color this beach ball is.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Red, white, and blue.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: How did you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: It was written on the box.

jokes.text: Latka correctly guessing the price: 'About ten dollars'
jokes.setup: Tony continues the guessing game with the price
jokes.punchline: Latka again guesses exactly right
jokes.explanation: Escalates the guessing gag, building audience expectation for the payoff

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Alright, Latka, I'm thinking of a number between one and a hundred.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Forty-two.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: How did you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Lucky guess.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, one more time. I'm thinking of a number between one and a thousand.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Seven hundred and thirty-eight.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: That's it! How are you doing this?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: In my country, we have very good schools.

jokes.text: Tony asking about the saleslady's height and Latka guessing 'Five foot three' then Tony's frustrated 'Not even close, Mr. Know-It-All'
jokes.setup: Pattern established of Latka guessing correctly
jokes.punchline: Finally Latka is wrong and Tony is triumphant
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the guessing gag - subverts expectation by having Latka finally be wrong, and Tony's relief/triumph is palpable

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Finally! You got one wrong!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: No, no, I was right.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You were not right! I'm telling you, you were wrong!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Tony, I know I was right.

jokes.text: Latka and Tony both claiming to be Bobby's best friend
jokes.setup: Latka says he's Bobby's best friend
jokes.punchline: Tony laughs and claims the same thing
jokes.explanation: Sets up character dynamics and potential conflict between Latka and Tony

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I'm Bobby's best friend.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'm Bobby's best friend.

jokes.text: Latka saying he'll return the beach ball 'to the six-feet tall saleslady'
jokes.setup: Earlier Tony said the saleslady wasn't five foot three
jokes.punchline: Latka now claims she was six feet tall
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier guessing game, showing Latka still doesn't know the right height

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: She was six feet tall!

jokes.text: Yeah, those people in, uh, California are pretty strange. Yeah. Yeah. Unlike here.
jokes.setup: Someone comments that California people are strange
jokes.punchline: Dry observation that New York taxi drivers calling others 'strange' is ironic
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan irony. Two words ('Unlike here') completely undercut the premise with brilliant economy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown character
dialogue.line: California people are strange

jokes.text: Jim accepting the welcome meant for Bobby: 'I didn't even know I was gone'
jokes.setup: Everyone welcomes who they think is Bobby
jokes.punchline: It's actually Jim, who plays along
jokes.explanation: Jim's characteristic zen acceptance of mistaken identity creates a sweet, absurd moment

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey everybody!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Bobby! Great to see you!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Welcome back, Bobby!

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Actually, it's Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Marty
dialogue.line: Jim? We thought you were Bobby.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Nope, just Jim. But I'll take the welcome anyway.

jokes.text: Jim saying 'I'm not one to nitpick' about his name being wrong
jokes.setup: Jim is told his name is Jim, not Bobby
jokes.punchline: He accepts this as a minor detail not worth arguing about
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jim's character - his laid-back attitude treats being called the wrong name as nitpicking

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, my name is Jim, not Bobby. Fine. I can live with that.

jokes.text: Bobby's assessment of California: 'Take away the beaches, the climate, the women, the glamour... still beats this'
jokes.setup: Listing all the good things about California and taking them away
jokes.punchline: Even without those things, it's still better than the garage
jokes.explanation: Great structure - builds up expectations then delivers a punchline that insults their workplace

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Even without those things, it's still better than the garage

jokes.text: Louie's backhanded welcome: 'If I'd have known you were coming, I'd have baked a ham'
jokes.setup: Louie appears to offer a warm welcome
jokes.punchline: It's clearly sarcastic and hostile
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - false hospitality that's clearly hostile, perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Welcome to my office. Please, sit down. Can I get you anything? Coffee? Tea? A new job somewhere else?

jokes.text: Louie's 'You never came here to work, Wheeler'
jokes.setup: Bobby says he didn't come back to work
jokes.punchline: Louie points out Bobby never worked when he was employed there
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that reveals character history and Louie's wit in a perfectly economical line

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? You didn't work when you were employed here!

jokes.text: Louie's Gettysburg joke: 'If he was at Gettysburg, we'd be singing Dixie before ball games'
jokes.setup: Louie claims everything Bobby touches is a failure
jokes.punchline: Absurd historical hypothetical about Bobby changing Civil War outcome
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation that takes Bobby's supposed failures to historical proportions. Original and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Everything you touch is a failure, Bobby. If you'd been around during the Civil War, the South would've won.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: How is that possible? I wasn't even born yet.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Exactly my point. You would've found a way.

jokes.text: Louie: 'If you held a gun to his head, Wheeler couldn't act nervous'
jokes.setup: Attacking Bobby's acting ability
jokes.punchline: Even in mortal danger he couldn't convincingly portray nervousness
jokes.explanation: Dark and creative insult about Bobby's acting that's both specific and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Even in mortal danger he couldn't convincingly portray nervousness

jokes.text: Louie: 'The highest priced seats are near the exits'
jokes.setup: When Bobby's in a play
jokes.punchline: People pay more to be able to escape quickly
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of theater economics - usually expensive seats are front row, here they're escape routes

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: When Bobby's in a play, people pay more to be able to escape quickly.

jokes.text: Louie: 'They have to put sick bags behind the seats'
jokes.setup: Continuing the theater insults
jokes.punchline: Bobby's acting is literally nauseating
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the theater gag, though slightly less creative than the previous lines

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Bobby's acting is literally nauseating

jokes.text: Louie: 'Couldn't I just take out my appendix with a warm spoon?'
jokes.setup: Asked to congratulate Bobby if he gets the series
jokes.punchline: Suggests painful self-surgery would be preferable
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific alternative that captures Louie's hatred perfectly. The 'warm spoon' detail makes it memorably grotesque

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Congratulate Bobby? I'd rather perform open heart surgery on myself with a rusty butter knife.

jokes.text: Louie: 'And then I'll go down and sprinkle rock salt where hell froze over'
jokes.setup: Agreeing to congratulate Bobby because it's impossible
jokes.punchline: Takes 'when hell freezes over' literally and offers to salt the ice
jokes.explanation: Clever literal interpretation of the idiom, though not as strong as the appendix line

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Congratulate Bobby? When hell freezes over!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I'll bring the salt.

jokes.text: What do you mean, 'pretty good'? It's fantastic. You know, if you hadn't said '150,000 bucks a week,' everybody'd be going crazy right now.
jokes.setup: Someone calls Bobby's salary 'pretty good' instead of fantastic
jokes.punchline: Points out that Bobby ruined the moment by setting expectations too high
jokes.explanation: Good meta-observation about how framing affects perception. Calls out Bobby's tactical error in the reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown character
dialogue.line: Your salary's pretty good.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby Baccalieri
dialogue.line: Pretty good? PRETTY GOOD?! It's fantastic! You just ruined the moment by setting expectations too high.

jokes.text: The celebration escalation from cab driver party to TV star party
jokes.setup: Bobby gets the series and wants to celebrate properly
jokes.punchline: Dismisses their current celebration as beneath his new status
jokes.explanation: Shows Bobby's character flaw of getting carried away with success, sets up for the fall

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Guys, guys, come on. This is no way to celebrate. I'm a star now. We need champagne, we need caviar, we need something classy.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Bobby, we got beer and pretzels.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Exactly. Beer and pretzels? That's for cabbies. I'm an actor. This is beneath me now.

jokes.text: Louie's drunken 'You ain't seen porfnig yet'
jokes.setup: Bobby's talking about real partying
jokes.punchline: Louie drunkenly slurs an attempt at 'partying' or similar word
jokes.explanation: Simple drunk comedy that shows Louie getting caught up in the celebration

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Partying... par... parsnipping...

jokes.text: Latka's earmuffs riddle: 'It keep you warm and it rhyme with zearmuffs'
jokes.setup: Latka gives a hint about his present
jokes.punchline: The hint basically gives away the answer through mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka logic - tries to be clever with riddle but accidentally makes it obvious through his accent

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I got you a nice kashmir.

jokes.text: The earmuffs revelation after Bobby guesses correctly
jokes.setup: Bobby correctly guesses 'earmuffs' from the obvious hint
jokes.punchline: Latka acts like it was a difficult riddle and reveals it proudly
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the riddle setup, showing Latka's innocence about how obvious his hint was

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You know, Bobby, I have to tell you something. That riddle, it took me three years to come up with it.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Three years?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Yes. I was very proud of it. I thought nobody in the whole world could ever guess it.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Well, I got it.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I know! That's what kills me!

jokes.text: Well, since you can't, could I have your juicer?
jokes.setup: Bobby makes heartfelt speech about wishing he could take everyone with him
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately asks for his juicer instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Bobby's sentimental moment. Great economy and timing - completely undercuts the emotion with practical greed.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: I wish I could take all of you with me.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You got a juicer?

jokes.text: Latka's reaction to buying the beach ball: 'I like to be laughed at by the clerks in the toy store'
jokes.setup: Latka bought Bobby a beach ball for California, now Bobby's staying
jokes.punchline: Latka sarcastically accepts being mocked by store employees
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the opening gag with Latka showing rare sarcasm about his gift purchases

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Is okay. I go back to store, they laugh at me, they point, they say 'Hey, look at the dummy who bought the beach ball!' Is fine. I don't mind. I come back tomorrow, they laugh again. Next week, same thing. Eventually, I become part of their routine. They start a betting pool on what stupid thing Latka will buy next. I don't care. This is America!

jokes.text: They said I wasn't sexy enough. What? I don't understand. They gotta be crazy. I mean, I ooze sex. I mean, look at my teeth and my hair, my height. I'm a sexy guy.
jokes.setup: Bobby reveals he was fired for not being sexy enough
jokes.punchline: His defensive list of 'sexy' attributes (teeth, hair, height) are mundane physical features
jokes.explanation: Bobby's earnest insistence that he 'oozes sex' followed by the most unsexy physical inventory is endearingly delusional.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: I'm sexy! I have great teeth!

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: I have nice hair!

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: I'm tall!

jokes.text: The waitress calling Bobby 'a sex god, señor'
jokes.setup: Bobby desperately asks a waitress if he's sexy
jokes.punchline: She gives an obviously fake, over-the-top answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff - the waitress gives Bobby exactly what he wants to hear in the most ridiculous way

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Am I sexy?

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Oh Bobby, you're the sexiest man alive! You're like a Greek god! Women swoon when you walk by! You make Adonis look like a troll!

jokes.text: Bobby's plan: 'I'm going to wander the streets aimlessly and ride the subways and babble to myself'
jokes.setup: Alex asks what Bobby's going to do now
jokes.punchline: Bobby describes becoming a stereotypical crazy homeless person
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny escalation that captures Bobby's despair through specific, visual details

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Well, I'm gonna let my hair grow real long, get a shopping cart, and push it around talking to myself about the government.

jokes.text: An old woman clubbing pigeons. No, no, not down there, up there. Oh, the sunrise.
jokes.setup: Jim asks Bobby what he sees out the window
jokes.punchline: Bobby first notices disturbing street scene before the inspiring sunrise Jim meant
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of the inspirational 'look at the sunrise' moment. The specific absurdity of 'clubbing pigeons' is memorable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What do you see out the window?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Well, there's a guy urinating on a fire hydrant, a woman going through the garbage cans, and a kid breaking into a car.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No, no, look up! See that beautiful sunrise?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Oh, that's nice.

jokes.text: Or the one after that.
jokes.setup: Bobby declares he'll be on tomorrow's noon flight to California
jokes.punchline: Adds his characteristic hedge of 'or the one after that'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bobby's earlier hesitation pattern. Shows he hasn't really changed despite the inspirational speech.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: I'll be on tomorrow's noon flight to California. Or the one after that.

jokes.text: Latka's gift confusion escalation: 'And then when I give you the beach ball, you're going to want the earmuffs?'
jokes.setup: Bobby keeps not needing Latka's gifts
jokes.punchline: Latka predicts an endless cycle of inappropriate gift exchanges
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Latka's frustration with his gift-giving, perfect character logic

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You see, Bobby, I give you gift, you don't need it. So next time I give you different gift, you don't need that either. This continue forever and ever. Is cycle of life, my friend. Latka give, Bobby no need. Latka give again, Bobby no need again. On and on, until one of us is dead.

jokes.text: I am fed up. I don't care where you go, what you do, when you do it, when you get there. I don't care. You're making me crazy. Earmuffs, beach ball. Earmuffs, beach ball...
jokes.setup: Latka continues his rant about the gift situation
jokes.punchline: Devolves into just repeating 'earmuffs, beach ball' while having a breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Latka's mounting frustration. The repetitive 'earmuffs, beach ball' creates a mantra of his breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Earmuffs, beach ball. Earmuffs, beach ball. Earmuffs, beach ball!

jokes.text: You probably are talented, because you're getting out of this place. It's something I gave up on a long time ago.
jokes.setup: Louie gives what seems like a genuine compliment to Bobby
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's really about his own self-pity and trapped situation
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly deep moment for Louie. What seems like kindness toward Bobby is really about his own resignation and trapped life.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: You know, Bobby, I gotta say something. You're a good guy. You got a good heart. You know what I mean? You're decent.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Thanks, Louie.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well don't thank me yet. You know what I wish? I wish I was you. I wish I could be like you, decent and good. But me? I'm stuck here. I'm trapped in this office, this job, this life. And you, you got out. You got away. So congratulations, Bobby. You're everything I'm not.

jokes.text: Louie's delayed reaction and then explosive laughter: 'That's the funniest thing I ever heard'
jokes.setup: Louie processes that he gave a sincere speech for nothing
jokes.punchline: His empathy immediately switches to cruel laughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect return to Louie's character after his moment of vulnerability - the delayed reaction makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... I gave a sincere speech and nobody's gonna remember it? Nobody's gonna care?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Ha ha ha ha! That's beautiful! That's beautiful!

jokes.text: Alex's final line: 'I think I'm gonna miss him the least'
jokes.setup: Louie is cackling cruelly at Bobby's misfortune
jokes.punchline: Alex dryly notes Louie will be the least missed
jokes.explanation: Good capper that shows Alex's dry wit in response to Louie's cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Louie, you're going to be the least missed.

jokes.text: Bobby's callback: 'Or the one after that' when talking about making it
jokes.setup: Tony says Bobby will make it on TV sooner than they think
jokes.punchline: Bobby repeats his earlier phrase about never giving up
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Bobby maintaining his optimism despite the setback

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You'll make it on TV sooner than you think.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: You really think so?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I know so.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: I'm never giving up.

jokes.text: Alex's final toast: 'Here's hoping we never see each other again'
jokes.setup: Alex explains that if Bobby becomes a star they'll never see each other again
jokes.punchline: He toasts to that outcome because it means Bobby succeeded
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bittersweet line that captures the friendship paradox - hoping for Bobby's success means hoping to lose him

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: If Bobby becomes a star, we'll never see each other again.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: So here's to Bobby becoming a star!

tmdb_overview: Bobby, who has moved to Hollywood, comes back to the garage for a visit and announces that he's filmed a pilot for a new prime-time soap opera. During his visit, he learns that the pilot has been picked up as a series and Bobby throws a party at the Plaza Hotel to celebrate. He soon learns that although the series is a go, they no longer want Bobby to appear in it. Bobby is upset and entertains the idea of moving back to New York, until Alex convinces him to follow his dream in Hollywood.
insight: Just shy of 80, this character-driven episode mines 36 jokes from Bobby's departure arc.
